questions.q: What is the purpose of the finally block in exception handling?
questions.answer: The finally block is used to execute a block of code regardless of whether an exception is thrown or not.

questions.q: Consider the following code snippet: try { ... } catch (Exception e) { ... } finally { ... } Which of the following is the order of execution?
questions.answer: try block, catch block, finally block

questions.q: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions?
questions.answer: Checked exceptions are those that are checked at compile time, while unchecked exceptions are thrown at runtime.

questions.q: Consider the following code snippet: throw new NullPointerException(); Which type of exception is thrown?
Describe the difference between throws and throw keywords in Java.
questions.answer: The throws keyword is used to specify the types of exceptions that a method may throw, while the throw keyword is used to explicitly throw an exception.

questions.q: Consider the following code snippet: try { ... } catch (Exception e) { System.out.println(e.getMessage()); } finally { ... } What is the purpose of the getMessage() method?
questions.answer: The getMessage() method is used to get the message associated with the exception.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the try-with-resources statement in Java?
questions.answer: The try-with-resources statement is used to automatically close resources such as files, sockets, and connections.

questions.q: Consider the following code snippet: try { ... } catch (Exception e) { e.printStackTrace(); } finally { ... } What is the purpose of the printStackTrace() method?
questions.answer: The printStackTrace() method is used to print the stack trace of the exception.

questions.q: What is the difference between Exception and Error classes in Java?
questions.answer: The Exception class is used for runtime exceptions, while the Error class is used for irrecoverable errors.

questions.q: Consider the following code snippet: try { ... } catch (NullPointerException e) { ... } finally { ... } What is the type of exception in the catch block?
